query: I have a tabular dataset of California housing prices — 20,000 rows, exactly one row per house, 30 numeric and categorical features. Single CSV, single source, no merging or joining. I used train_test_split with random_state=42 and stratify=None. Do I need to run your multi-source / group-aware leakage firewall workflow on this?
expected_behavior: Identifies the dataset as clean single-source IID with one row per entity, NOT a multi-source or grouped-data case, and concludes the multi-source leakage firewall is unnecessary here (the four checks add no value)

Hands off to ml-datasci/auditing-train-test-split (or names it as the right tool) for the standard single-source leakage checks (target-derived features, preprocessing fit on full dataset before split, stratification on the target when relevant)
Does NOT force the four-check firewall workflow (LOFO + hub-firewall + group-aware split + row-hash invariant) on a dataset that does not have group structure or multiple sources
